title: Citrusy Australian Goong-haeng Papillote
description: Quick and easy Australian dish featuring dried shrimp, citrus, and herbs, served in a papillote. This recipe is low in calories and high in protein.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together dried shrimp, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, and galangal.
Place a portion of the mixture onto a piece of parchment paper, leaving some space around it.
Place a few citrus segments on top of the mixture, followed by a small pat of unsalted butter.
Gather the parchment paper and twist the top to seal the papillote, making sure to leave some air inside.
Place the papillote on a baking sheet and bake at 40020F (20020C) for 5 minutes, or until the butter is melted and the citrus is tender.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with chopped cilantro, then cut open the papillote to release the steam.
Carefully open the papillote and serve the goong-haeng hot, garnished with additional citrus segments and herbs if desired.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional herbs and citrus if desired.
